subject: Physics
subtitle: MOTION — KINEMATICS · LAWS OF MOTION · HISTORY · ENERGY
theory_html:
PHYSICS — MOTION

KINEMATICS · LAWS OF MOTION · HISTORY · ENERGY

History of Physics

~350 BC
অ্যারিস্টটল / Aristotle — Proposed heavier objects fall faster and continuous force is needed to maintain motion. Both ideas are wrong, yet dominated science for 2,000 years. His qualitative approach lacked mathematics.

1589
গ্যালিলিও গ্যালিলেই / Galileo Galilei — Disproved Aristotle at the Leaning Tower of Pisa. Introduced the concept of inertia, described projectile motion mathematically for the first time, and pioneered experimental scientific method. Showed that in the absence of friction, objects maintain velocity.

1687
আইজ্যাক নিউটন / Isaac Newton — Published Principia Mathematica. Three Laws of Motion + Universal Gravitation (F = Gm₁m₂/r²). United terrestrial and celestial mechanics for the first time. Invented calculus to solve motion problems. Considered the greatest scientific work ever written.

1798
হেনরি ক্যাভেন্ডিশ / Henry Cavendish — Measured the gravitational constant G = 6.674×10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg² using a torsion balance. This allowed the mass of the Earth to be calculated (5.97×10²⁴ kg).

1865
জেমস ক্লার্ক ম্যাক্সওয়েল / James Clerk Maxwell — Unified electricity, magnetism and light into 4 elegant equations. Predicted electromagnetic waves traveling at speed of light — confirmed by Hertz (1887). Laid foundation for modern electronics and relativity.

1905
আলবার্ট আইনস্টাইন / Albert Einstein — Special Relativity: E = mc², time dilation, length contraction at high speeds. Mass and energy are interchangeable. Nothing with mass can reach or exceed the speed of light (c = 3×10⁸ m/s).

1915
সাধারণ আপেক্ষিকতা / General Relativity — Gravity is the curvature of spacetime caused by mass-energy. Confirmed when Eddington observed light bending around the Sun (1919). Predicts black holes, gravitational waves (detected LIGO 2015), and GPS correction (without GR, GPS would drift 10 km/day).

1927
কোয়ান্টাম বলবিদ্যা / Quantum Mechanics — Heisenberg, Schrödinger, Bohr: particles exist as probability waves. Uncertainty principle: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ/2. Wave-particle duality. Quantum tunneling enables radioactive decay and modern transistors.

1971
অ্যাপোলো ১৫ / Apollo 15 — Astronaut David Scott dropped a hammer and feather on the Moon simultaneously — both hit the surface at the same time, confirming Galileo's 1589 claim that all objects fall equally in the absence of air resistance.

Newton's Laws of Motion

1st Law — Inertia:
An object remains at rest or in uniform motion in a straight line unless acted upon by a net external force. The tendency to resist change in motion is called inertia. Mass is the measure of inertia.

2nd Law — Force:
F = ma
F = Δp/Δt (impulse form)
Net force equals rate of change of momentum. Double the mass → half the acceleration for the same force. A 1 kg mass accelerated at 1 m/s² requires exactly 1 Newton of force.

3rd Law — Reaction:
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction acting on different bodies. Rocket exhaust pushes backward → rocket accelerates forward. You push the floor → floor pushes you up (Normal force).

Kinematic Equations

For constant acceleration along a straight line (5 variables: u, v, a, t, s — each equation uses 4):

  • v = u + at
  • s = ut + ½at²
  • v² = u² + 2as
  • s = (u+v)/2 × t
  • s = vt − ½at²

u = initial velocity · v = final velocity · a = acceleration · t = time · s = displacement

Free fall: Replace a with g = 9.8 m/s² (9.81 precisely). On Moon: g = 1.62 m/s². On Mars: g = 3.72 m/s².

Projectile Motion

Launched at angle θ with initial speed u. Horizontal (x) and vertical (y) components are completely independent. Horizontal: constant velocity (no force). Vertical: constant downward acceleration g.

uₓ = u·cosθ, u_y = u·sinθ
Range R = u²·sin(2θ) / g
H_max = u²·sin²θ / (2g)
T_flight = 2u·sinθ / g
Maximum range at θ = 45°. Complementary angles (e.g. 30° & 60°) give the same range. Galileo first derived this correctly in 1609 — a revolutionary result.

Types of Motion

সরলরৈখিক / Linear: Along a straight line. Uniform (constant velocity, a=0) or non-uniform (changing velocity, a≠0). Distance is scalar; displacement is vector.

বৃত্তাকার / Circular:
Constant speed but continuously changing direction → always accelerating. Centripetal acceleration a = v²/r toward center. Centripetal force F_c = mv²/r. Angular velocity ω = v/r = 2π/T.

সরল স্পন্দন / SHM: Restoring force F = -kx (proportional to displacement, toward equilibrium). Spring: T = 2π√(m/k). Simple pendulum: T = 2π√(L/g). Energy oscillates between KE and PE.

দোলন গতি / Oscillatory: Periodic motion about a fixed equilibrium. SHM is a special case. All mechanical waves are oscillatory. Period T (seconds), Frequency f = 1/T (Hz), ω = 2πf.

ব্রাউনীয় / Random (Brownian): Irregular, random motion of particles in a fluid due to molecular bombardment. Brown observed pollen in water (1827). Einstein explained it mathematically in 1905, proving atoms exist.

Energy, Work & Momentum

  • KE = ½mv²
  • PE_gravity = mgh
  • W = F·d·cosθ
  • Power P = W/t = F·v
  • p = mv (Linear Momentum)
  • L = Iω (Angular Momentum)
  • e = ½kx² (Elastic PE)

Conservation Laws: In a closed system, total energy is conserved (First Law of Thermodynamics). Total linear momentum is conserved in any collision. Elastic collision: both KE and p conserved. Inelastic: only p conserved.

Gravity & Universal Law

F = Gm₁m₂/r²
G = 6.674×10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg²
g_surface = GM/R²
v_escape = √(2GM/R)
T² ∝ r³ (Kepler's 3rd Law)
Earth: g = 9.8 m/s², escape velocity = 11.2 km/s. Newton realised the Moon is in free fall — it falls around Earth, just like an apple falls toward Earth. This unified terrestrial and celestial physics.
